key_insight: For intermetallic NRR catalysts, s- and p-band features can be more predictive than the traditional d-band center, especially for N2 and N2H adsorption.
engineering_relevance: This provides a compact, ML-ready descriptor set for screening non-noble intermetallics and accelerating catalyst design for sustainable ammonia synthesis.
method: Built a dataset of 47 bimetallic intermetallic compounds with ~1,200 DFT adsorption data points for N2, N2H, and NH3 across surface sites.
Trained machine learning models using intrinsic properties, s/p/d-band centers and fillings, and neighboring-atom Bader charges.
Used SHAP analysis to rank the most influential descriptors and test the d-band-centric assumption.
evidence_signal: The models achieved mean absolute errors of 0.26 eV for N2, 0.39 eV for N2H, and 0.17 eV for NH3 using only 20 key features.
watch_item: The descriptor ranking is derived from a relatively limited set of intermetallics, so transferability to wider catalyst families and real operating conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Going Beyond the d-band Center to Design Intermetallic Catalysts for Nitrogen Reduction: A High-Throughput DFT and Machine Learning Study
